title: Gamandar
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{CelestialBody |image=[[File:WelcometoGamandar-MSW75.jpg]] |name=Gamandar |region=*[[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} *[[The Slice/Legends|The Slice]]The [[:File:MiscellaneousRegions.jpg|Client States and Miscellaneous Regions map]] in ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' places the entirety of grid square R-16 in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]]. |sector=[[Trans-Nebular sector/Legends|Trans-Nebular sector]] |system=[[Iskalon system/Legends|Iskalon system]] |coordinates=R-16 |xyz= |distance= |suns= |orbited= |position= |moons= |routes= |lengthday= |lengthyear= |class= |diameter= |atmosphere= |climate= |gravity= |terrain=Rocky[[Star Wars (1977) 74|''Star Wars'' (1977) 74]] |water= |interest=[[Gamandar Citadel]] |flora= |fauna= |otherlife= |species=[[Gamandar (species)|Gamandars]] {{C|[[near-Human]]}} |otherspecies=[[Human/Legends|Humans]] |socialgroup= |language=[[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] |government= |population= |demonym= |cities= |imports= |exports= |affiliation=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] }} {{Quote|Our twin planet, Gamandar, was totally subjugated. The Empire has built an enormous fortress there and completely enslaved the populace.|[[Primor (Inleshat)|Primor]]|}} '''Gamandar''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim's]] [[Iskalon system/Legends|Iskalon system]], considered the twin [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of the nearby [[Water/Legends|waterworld]] of [[Iskalon/Legends|Iskalon]]. The [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of a [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] also known as the [[Gamandar (species)|Gamandars]], the planet was brutally conquered and its people [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Afterwards, [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Griggor Tower]] established his base of operations in the [[Star system/Legends|system]] in a massive [[Gamandar Citadel|citadel]] on Gamandar. ==Description== {{Quote|These Gamandars seem like a pretty feeble bunch..."
"Once they're transported, they'll make a good enough work force...|Two Imperial stormtroopers|}} A rocky [[Planet/Legends|world]] in the [[Iskalon system/Legends|Iskalon system]] of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]], Gamandar was one of three habitable worlds in its [[Star system/Legends|star system]], joining its "twin" of [[Iskalon/Legends|Iskalon]] as well as [[Telfrey]]. Gamandar was home to a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of the [[Gamandar (species)|same name]], a race of [[Color/Legends|red]]-skinned [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]]. Gamandar's ecosystem was almost completely destroyed during the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] occupation of the planet, as anything natural was wantonly replaced by machinery.[[Star Wars (1977) 75|''Star Wars'' (1977) 75]] ==History== {{Quote|I offered to come along with you because this world, Gamandar, twin to my own Iskalon, is familiar to me... I thought I could help you blend in, and show you the local ways and places... but ''this'' Gamandar I no longer know!|[[Mone]]|}} During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Gamandar became a major flashpoint in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] subjugation of the [[Iskalon system/Legends|Iskalon system]]. Forces led by [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Griggor Tower]] conquered the planet and [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] the populace, while also occupying Iskalon and [[Decimation of Telfrey|bombarding]] Telfrey. Tower raised a massive [[Gamandar Citadel|citadel]] in the aftermath, which became the base from which he ruled the system. The Gamandar natives were put in chains and rounded up to be shipped offworld to serve on work crews, while the planet's ecosystem was devastated—for some [[Iskalonian]] visitors, the planet had been rendered almost completely unrecognizable. It was from Gamandar that Tower launched a [[Missile/Legends|missile]] strike in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] that triggered the [[Iskalon Effect]], a massive tidal wave that devastated Iskalonian society, after learning that Gamandar's twin world was harboring a cell of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] agents. However, one of those Rebels was [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], a highly desired personal target of the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], who ordered Tower put to [[Death/Legends|death]] for risking Skywalker's life.[[Star Wars (1977) 76|''Star Wars'' (1977) 76]] ==Behind the scenes== Gamandar appeared as a setting of three issues of the original [[Star Wars (1977)|Marvel ''Star Wars'']] comic series in [[1983]], written by [[Mary Jo Duffy]] and illustrated by [[Ron Frenz]]. ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars (1977) 74|''Star Wars'' (1977) 74]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 74}} {{1st}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 75|''Star Wars'' (1977) 75]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 75}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 76|''Star Wars'' (1977) 76]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 76}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 77|''Star Wars'' (1977) 77]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 77}} {{Mo}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 82|''Star Wars'' (1977) 82]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 82}} {{Mo}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 85|''Star Wars'' (1977) 85]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 85}} {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Gamandar }} [[Category:Galactic Empire planets]] [[Category:Iskalon system locations]] [[Category:Planets in the Mid Rim]] [[Category:Planets in the Slice]] [[Category:Rocky planets]]
